[Thu 2026-04-23 01:07 GMT+8] [Subagent Context] You are running as a subagent (depth 1/1). Results auto-announce to your requester; do not busy-poll for status.

[Subagent Task]: You are a research agent for Project Arjun — an India-wide geospatial intelligence platform.

Create a document called `research-open-data-integrations.md` in the `gods-eye/` directory.

Deep research into OPEN SOURCE data integrations specifically for India-focused government monitoring. Focus on data that is ACTUALLY available and usable (not theoretical).

## Research These Data Categories:

### 1. Satellite Data (Free)
- Sentinel-2 (10m resolution, 5-day revisit)
- Landsat (30m, 16-day)
- MODIS (250m-1km, daily)
- NASA FIRMS (fire detection, near-real-time)
- ISRO Resourcesat (Indian satellite, how to access)
- How to compute NDVI, NDWI, NDBI for agriculture/water/urban monitoring
- Google Earth Engine scripts for India-specific analysis
- Practical: How to detect crop health, water bodies, urban sprawl

### 2. Weather & Climate
- IMD API (real endpoints, not just website)
- Open-Meteo (free, no key, supports India)
- OpenWeatherMap free tier
- Rainfall data for flood prediction
- Heatwave/cold wave alert data
- Practical: How to overlay weather on the map in real-time

### 3. Demographics
- Census 2011 data (village level, how to download and parse)
- Voter rolls (Election Commission, format)
- NFHS (National Family Health Survey) microdata
- SECC (Socio-Economic Caste Census) data
- Practical: How to join census data with GIS boundaries

### 4. Health
- HMIS (Health Management Information System) portal
- IDSP (Integrated Disease Surveillance Programme)
- CoWIN data (vaccination, now public?)
- Hospital/PHC locations (from data.gov.in)
- Practical: Disease outbreak mapping

### 5. Education
- UDISE+ (school data, location, infrastructure)
- Mid-day meal data
- ASER (Annual Status of Education Report)
- Practical: School infrastructure gap analysis

### 6. Agriculture
- Crop production data (DAC&FW)
- Soil health card data
- PMFBY (crop insurance) claims
- Market price data (AGMARKNET)
- Practical: Crop health monitoring dashboard

### 7. Water
- CGWB groundwater levels
- Water quality data (CPCB)
- Dam/reservoir levels (CWC)
- Jal Jeevan Mission progress
- Practical: Water stress mapping

### 8. Infrastructure
- PMGSY road data
- NHAI project status
- Railway station locations
- Airport data
- Practical: Infrastructure development tracking

### 9. Finance
- OpenBudgetsIndia
- State budget documents
- PFMS expenditure data
- MGNREGA wage payment data
- Practical: Budget vs expenditure comparison

### 10. Crime & Safety
- NCRB data
- eCourts case data
- Accidental deaths data
- Practical: Crime hotspot mapping

For EACH category:
1. Primary data source URL
2. API/download format
3. Update frequency
4. Data quality assessment (how reliable)
5. How to integrate into CesiumJS
6. Example visualization
7. Known issues/gaps

Focus on sources that DON'T require government permission or paid APIs. Everything should be accessible to a citizen developer.

The Census 2011 is the 15th National census survey conducted by the Census Organization of India. Mr. C. Chandramouli is the Commissioner & Registrar General of the Indian 2011 Census. The 2011 Indian National Census has been conducted in 2 phases - house listing and population. The national census survey covered all the 28 states of the country and 7 Union territories including 640 districts, 497 cities, 5767 tehsils & over 6 lakh villages.

 The Indian Population Census 2011 covered a number of parameters during the survey. These parameters include population, growth rate in population, rate of literacy, density of population, sex ratio and child sex ratio (0-6 years). According to the census reports of Indian Census 2011, the population of India is 1,210,854,977 with 623, 724, 248 males and 586,469, 174 females. The total literacy rate in the country at present is 74.04%. The density of population is 382 persons/sq.km. In regards to sex ratio, at present there are 940 females on average on per 1000 males and the child sex ratio is 914 females per 1000 males.

 The Indian Census survey is conducted to gather information from the grass root base which is essential to launch different welfare schemes like Annual Plans, 5 Year Plans etc. in the country for the privilege of common man here. The Houselisting & Housing Census provides extensive information on the status of the human settlements, the housing deficits as well as the different housing necessities to be looked for formulating the housing welfare policies.

 The Population Enumeration offers needed Census data about land & its people in the present time. The survey reveals the current population trends, its varied characteristics that are valuable inputs for planning sound programs and policies aimed towards the welfare of India & her people and also for effective public administration.

#### Description

 See also collection COPERNICUS/S2_SR_HARMONIZED that shifts data with
PROCESSING_BASELINE '04.00' or above (after 2022-01-25) to be in the same range
as in older scenes.

Sentinel-2 is a wide-swath, high-resolution, multi-spectral
imaging mission supporting Copernicus Land Monitoring studies,
including the monitoring of vegetation, soil and water cover,
as well as observation of inland waterways and coastal areas.

The Sentinel-2 L2 data are downloaded from CDSE. They were
computed by running sen2cor. WARNING: 2017-2018 L2 coverage
in the EE collection is not yet global.

The assets contain
12 UINT16 spectral bands representing SR scaled by 10000 (unlike in L1 data,
there is no B10). There are also several more L2-specific bands (see band
list for details). See the
[Sentinel-2 User Handbook](https://sentinel.esa.int/documents/247904/685211/Sentinel-2_User_Handbook)
for details.

QA60 is a bitmask band that contained rasterized cloud mask
polygons until 2022-01-25, when these polygons stopped being produced.
Starting 2024-02-28, legacy-consistent QA60 bands are constructed from
the MSK_CLASSI cloud classification bands.
For more details,
[see the full explanation of how cloud masks are computed.](https://sentinel.esa.int/web/sentinel/technical-guides/sentinel-2-msi/level-1c/cloud-masks)

EE asset ids for Sentinel-2 L2 assets have the following format:
COPERNICUS/S2_SR/20151128T002653_20151128T102149_T56MNN. Here the
first numeric part represents the sensing date and time, the
second numeric part represents the product generation date and
time, and the final 6-character string is a unique granule identifier
indicating its UTM grid reference (see [MGRS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Military_Grid_Reference_System)).

For datasets to assist with cloud and/or cloud shadow detection, see [COPERNICUS/S2_CLOUD_PROBABILITY](/earth-engine/datasets/catalog/COPERNICUS_S2_CLOUD_PROBABILITY)
and [GOOGLE/CLOUD_SCORE_PLUS/V1/S2_HARMONIZED](/earth-engine/datasets/catalog/GOOGLE_CLOUD_SCORE_PLUS_V1_S2_HARMONIZED).

For more details on Sentinel-2 radiometric resolution, [see this page](https://sentinel.esa.int/web/sentinel/user-guides/sentinel-2-msi/resolutions/radiometric).
